programs.name: Guardian Swim 3
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Develop all skills unassisted. Learn front swim with basic arm action and jump into the water with a personal flotation device (PFD). One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Introduction to Airbrush
programs.description: Explore airbrushing as a creative tool for visual expression in this 1-day workshop. Using a practice-based approach, your instructor will help you create your own airbrush paintings with informative talks and demonstrations. programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Bronze Cross Exam:Sunday, May 31st, 20265:30pm - 8:30pm Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Cross and Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving  Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving  to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and  begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic  facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and  communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross  is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and  leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres  in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Standard First Aid (CPR C)  certification provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal  injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest  injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external  defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in  Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with  CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.  Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual  received in Bronze Medallion. For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds  for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Cross and Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving  Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving  to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and  begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic  facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and  communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross  is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and  leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres  in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Standard First Aid (CPR C)  certification provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal  injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest  injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external  defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in  Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with  CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.  Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual  received in Bronze Medallion. For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds  for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Qigong
programs.description: Take part in a series of mind and body exercises to improve your flexibility, strength and posture. With focused intention and relaxed movement, you will learn how to integrate breathing and meditation into your wellness routine.

programs.name: Ready, Set, PLAY! with Caregiver
programs.description: Before you can throw out a first pitch, you need to learn how to throw a ball. In Ready, Set PLAY! you will learn the basics of moving confidently and competently in a fun and active environment. What you will develop here, you will apply to a lifetime of participation in a variety of sports and activities. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: This is an introductory instructional program that introduces children to basic active play to develop physical literacy. Through a variety of activities in a social and fun recreational environment, children will develop fundamental movement (e.g. run, jump, skip, hop) and creative skills. Children will participate in activities including: theme-based physical activities, games, arts and crafts, and unstructured play.  A Caregiver must accompany the child. Staff provide supervision and instruction.

programs.name: Qigong
programs.description: Take part in a series of mind and body exercises to improve your flexibility, strength and posture. With focused intention and relaxed movement, you will learn how to integrate breathing and meditation into your wellness routine.

programs.name: Ready, Set, PLAY! with Caregiver
programs.description: Before you can throw out a first pitch, you need to learn how to throw a ball. In Ready, Set PLAY! you will learn the basics of moving confidently and competently in a fun and active environment. What you will develop here, you will apply to a lifetime of participation in a variety of sports and activities. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends.

programs.name: Recreation Adventures with Caregiver
programs.description: This is an introductory instructional program that introduces children to basic active play to develop physical literacy. Through a variety of activities in a social and fun recreational environment, children will develop fundamental movement (e.g. run, jump, skip, hop) and creative skills. Children will participate in activities including: theme-based physical activities, games, arts and crafts, and unstructured play.  A Caregiver must accompany the child. Staff provide supervision and instruction.

programs.name: SPLASH Swim Team
programs.description: Expand your swimming skills by practicing your strokes in a team-based environment and showcase your skills during competitive swim meets. Prerequisite: Must be able to swim at least two continuous pool lengths (approximately 50 metres) of unassisted front swim and demonstrate ability to take breaths. IMPORTANT: The City of Toronto is dedicated to ensuring the safety of all individuals who participate in its activities and services. This program requires the review of Ontario's Concussion Awareness Resources and the City of Toronto's Concussion Code of Conduct. A signature of acknowledgment is required for participants under the age of 26, or parents/caregivers of participants under the age of 18, on the first day of program participation.Visit www.toronto.ca/concussionsafety for more information on concussion safety in our sports and recreation programs.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion, Cross & Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This is an intensive program designed for strong swimmers. The Bronze Medallion certification teaches how to manage complex water rescues through the development of physical fitness and judgment skills. The Bronze Cross certification teaches the difference between lifesaving and lifeguarding, the principles of emergency procedures and teamwork. The Standard First Aid (CPR C) certification provides comprehensive first aid training that covers spinal injuries, heat or cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and automated external defibrillator usage. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: Zumba Gold®
programs.description: Latin dance and music inspire this fun and energetic cardio workout. Using low-impact steps and aerobic movements, you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Cardio: Kickboxing
programs.description: Calling all fitness warriors. This high-intensity cardio workout that incorporates non-contact punches, kicks and athletic moves will help you improve your strength, balance and coordination.

programs.name: Gymnastics with Caregiver
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on fun and safety, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Strength and Conditioning
programs.description: Get your muscles moving in the Strength and Conditioning class. With an emphasis on full body conditioning, exercises will target key muscle groups including arms, legs, abdominals and chest to improve you overall endurance and flexibility.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Cooking: Vegetarian with Caregiver
programs.description: Get creative with great ingredients like fruits, vegetables and grains. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain comfort in the kitchen. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Dance: Belly Dance
programs.description: Known for its energetic and expressive movements, belly dancing engages the whole body. Learn to use your hips, hands and torso through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Drawing
programs.description: Bring your imagination to life on paper. Learn the basics of how to draw using shapes and colours to create pictures with markers, crayons and pencil crayons.

programs.name: Drawing and Painting

programs.name: Adapted Counsellor in Training
programs.description: Becoming a camp counsellor is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. In this camp, you will learn about program planning and working with campers in an interactive environment. You will also get the opportunity to do a supervised practical placement in a City of Toronto summer camp and receive a certificate upon completion.As this is a registered activity, participants will not receive credit for volunteer hours. For more information on volunteer opportunities, contact your local community centre.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Making Music
programs.description: As you clap, sing, laugh and play you will discover making music is fun. Through a variety of activities, crafts and games you will learn the basics of rhythm and sounds.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adapted Recreation

programs.name: Challenge Swim: Bronze
programs.description: Take on the challenge of improving your fitness and endurance. In this first level of the Challenge Swim program you will learn backstroke starts and somersaults for flip turns while continuing to work on interval training and swimming drills. Work towards a 200-metre continuous swim in a non-competitive environment where you will aim to improve your swim times and skills.Prerequisite: Successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7.

programs.name: Challenge Swim: Gold
programs.description: Take on the challenge of improving your fitness and endurance. In this third and final level of the Challenge Swim program you will learn relay starts while continuing to work on interval training and swimming drills. Work towards a 500-metre continuous swim in a non-competitive environment, where you will aim to improve your swim times and skills.Prerequisite: Successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7 or Challenge Silver.

programs.name: Challenge Swim: Silver
programs.description: Take on the challenge of improving your fitness and endurance. In this second level of the Challenge Swim program, you will learn about relay dynamics, breaststroke and butterfly starts while continuing to work on flip and stroke turns, interval training and swimming drills. Work towards a 400-metre continuous swim in a non-competitive environment where you will aim to improve your swim times and skills.Prerequisite: Successful completion or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 7 or Challenge Bronze.

programs.description: Explore 19th century craftsmanship and design. Discover your talents with weaving, stitching, crafting and more.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Secret Garden at Spadina Museum
programs.description: Be immersed in the beautiful gardens to learn about nature, plants, wildlife and the history of this land. Using seasonal produce of the kitchen garden, make delicious treats in the kitchen of the historic coach house. Inspired by nature and the beautiful art in the museum, this camp has plenty of arts and crafts for children to unleash their imagination and creativity to express themselves through paint, clay, collage and more.  Campers bring their curiosity and travel back in time to the 1920s to discover the secrets of the house and uncover amazing and surprising history.  In addition to the extensive outdoor space, campers have a cool and comfortable home base in the and make special visits throughout the historic mansion as well.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Todmorden Mills
programs.description: CampTO at Todmorden MillsExperience unique history and natural landscape through special guided tours of the Wildflower Preserve, Papermill Gallery and two historic homes. This camp features standard camp activities including outdoor play, crafts and activities inspired by the nature and history of the valley.

programs.name: CampTO Plus Museums: Todmorden Mills
programs.description: CampTO at Todmorden MillsCampTO at Todmorden Mills invites campers to immerse themselves in the nature and history of the Don Valley.  Experience the unique history and natural landscape of Todmorden Mills through special guided tours of the Wildflower Preserve, Papermill Gallery, and the two historic homes. This camp features standard camp activities including outdoor play, crafts, and activities inspired by the nature and history of the valley.

programs.name: Gymnastics
programs.description: Flexibility, strength, coordination and balance, gymnastics has it all. With a focus on fun and safety, you will learn and practice basic skills such as walks, jumps, rolls and turns on various apparatuses.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination. In soccer you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Soccer: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this soccer activity, you will learn basics like kicking, passing and heading as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Tennis: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational tennis. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Adapted Making Music
programs.description: As you clap, sing, laugh and play you will discover making music is fun. Through a variety of activities, crafts and games you will learn the basics of rhythm and sounds.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Badminton: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In badminton, a racquet sport that focuses on hand-eye coordination and has you using your racquet to rally for points with a ‘birdie’ you will learn basics like grip, serve and swing, as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Ball Hockey: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this non-ice version of hockey played with a ball, you will learn basics like passing, shooting and stick handling as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Equipment: Players can bring a CSA-approved helmet and hockey gloves.

programs.name: Basketball: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Cartooning
programs.description: Learn the various styles of cartooning. As you practice and perfect basic drawing skills you will move into illustrating scenes, demonstrating motion and creating characters with expressive faces that you can incorporate into single-box and multi-boxes cartoons.

programs.name: Cartooning: Anime and Manga
programs.description: Learn about the Japanese style of cartooning and comic books called Anime and Manga. As you practice and perfect basic drawing skills you will move into illustrating scenes, demonstrating motion and creating characters with expressive faces in the unique style.

programs.name: Club: Reading
programs.description: If you love books and reading, you will love this activity. Not only will you enjoy your favourite book, but you can share ideas and thoughts about your reading with others. This activity is facilitated by staff, there is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Want to liven up the kitchen table? Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Crafty Creations
programs.description: Are you ready to create? From making and cutting patterns to choosing colours and decorative elements, you will use a variety of arts and crafts materials, your hands and your imagination, to make unique craft projects.
programs.location: Topham Park Cmty Centre and Clubhouse

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

In basketball, you will learn basics like passing, dribbling, layups and shooting as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Minimum age is 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.

programs.name: Club: Social

programs.name: Computers: Web Design

Participants should have basic computer skills and understand how to navigate the internet.Equipment: Use of computers vary by location, please check ahead. You may bring a laptop.

Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Cooking
programs.description: Want to liven up the kitchen table? Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.
